mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in 55% of rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and supports positive equity market performance over one month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 20-day return direction in at least 50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which depresses earnings expectations and causes negative quarterly equity returns during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of cases during identified contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Lower inflation readings reduce real interest rates and ease expectations of central bank tightening, providing immediate liquidity tailwinds that push equity prices higher over the following week.
failure_condition: Declines in CPIAUCSL are associated with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annually signals insufficient economic momentum to sustain corporate profit margins, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing away from equities and generating negative monthly returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of quarters where annualized GDPC1 growth is below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive quarterly equity performance.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE yields negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates rapidly improving capacity utilization and corporate output, which translates into strong earnings revisions and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery regimes fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 50% of cases.
